message.content: the test is failing again. TypeError: cannot read property 'name' of undefined at line 42. Let me check the logs more carefully.

content.text: What does the stack trace say exactly? Can you share the full error output?

message.content: it says the user object is undefined. But I'm setting it right before. Let me add a console.log to check.

content.text: Good idea. Log it just before the failing line and we'll see what state it's in.

message.content: okay it shows undefined. that's weird. the function is definitely getting called. maybe it's an async issue?

content.text: Could be. Is there an await missing somewhere in the call chain?

message.content: let me look at the call chain. I see getUserById is async. And I'm not awaiting it. Let me add await.

content.text: That's likely it. Add the await and re-run the test.

message.content: still failing. same error different line now. line 87 this time. TypeError: cannot read property 'email' of undefined.

content.text: Different property, same pattern — another undefined object. What's on line 87?

message.content: it's accessing session.email. the session variable. let me log that too. this is getting tedious.

content.text: Log the whole session object just after you assign it. Should tell us what's coming back.

message.content: session is also undefined. the getSession function must be returning null or something. let me check its return value.

content.text: Check if there's a conditional return or early exit in getSession that you might be hitting.

message.content: yeah found it. there's an early return when the token is expired. the test token is expired apparently. generating a new one.

content.text: Makes sense. Refresh the token and re-run.

message.content: still getting an error but different now. cannot read property 'role' of undefined at line 103. how many of these are there.

content.text: Sounds like a cascade of the same root problem — undefined objects throughout. Check if the whole auth flow is working end to end.

message.content: I think the test setup is broken. the beforeEach hook might not be running properly. let me check the test file.

content.text: Check if beforeEach is defined at the right describe scope. Sometimes nesting issues cause hooks to not fire.

message.content: you're right it's in the wrong describe block. moving it up one level. re-running now.

content.text: That should fix the cascade. Run it and see.

message.content: three tests pass now but two still fail. the ones involving role checks. totally different area of the code.

content.text: What error are those two showing?

message.content: TypeError again. this time on line 201. cannot read property 'permissions' of undefined. the role object.

content.text: Is the role being seeded in the test database? The role object might not exist at all.

message.content: checking the seed file. yeah the roles table isn't being populated in test setup. that's the problem. let me add the seed.

content.text: Add the seed data and make sure it runs before the tests.

message.content: okay added it. running again. now I get a different error: duplicate key violation on the roles insert. already exists somehow.

content.text: The table probably isn't being cleared between test runs. Add a delete or truncate before the insert.

message.content: added truncate. all tests passing now. took two hours to find a missing beforeEach scope and a missing seed. exhausting.

content.text: Classic. At least it's green now.

message.content: now a completely different test is red. the integration test for the payment handler. TypeError: cannot read property 'amount' of undefined at line 317.

content.text: What's the test doing? Is it mocking the payment response or hitting a real endpoint?

message.content: it's mocking. the mock returns a payment object but apparently the handler isn't getting it. let me log inside the handler.

content.text: Log the raw argument coming into the handler and see if it matches the mock shape.

message.content: the handler is getting undefined for the whole payment arg. the mock isn't being called at all it seems.

content.text: Is the mock being set up before the function is imported, or after? Import order can break mocks in Jest.

message.content: oh. the import is at the top, mock is in beforeEach. let me move the mock to the top of the file with jest.mock.

content.text: Yes, jest.mock is hoisted automatically so it needs to be at module scope.

message.content: moved it. still broken. now getting: jest.mock is not a function. I'm not using jest actually I'm using node:test.

content.text: Right, node:test has a different mocking API. Use mock.module or mock.fn from the node:test module.

message.content: okay found the docs. using mock.fn now. the mock is being called but returns undefined still. I forgot to set the return value.

content.text: Set mockReturnValue or mockImplementation with the payment object you want returned.

message.content: got it working. mock.fn().mockReturnValue works. test passes. that was another hour for a missing mock return value.

content.text: These things always take longer than they should. All green now?
